title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description: The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that is designed to process large data sets across clusters of computers. It is composed of two main components: the Map component and the Reduce component. The Map component breaks down the input data into smaller chunks and applies a user-defined function to each chunk. The Reduce component then aggregates the results from the Map component and produces the final output. The architecture is scalable, fault-tolerant, and provides a high degree of flexibility and extensibility. It is built on top of the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and uses the Java programming language.
raw_response:
TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture

DESCRIPTION:
The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that is designed to process large data sets across clusters of computers. It is composed of two main components: the Map component and the Reduce component. The Map component breaks down the input data into smaller chunks and applies a user-defined function to each chunk. The Reduce component then aggregates the results from the Map component and produces the final output. The architecture is scalable, fault-tolerant, and provides a high degree of flexibility and extensibility. It is built on top of the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and uses the Java programming language.
